36.reason: [Uninitialized variable: p->h is used uninitialized. When a new node is created, its height 'h' must be set to the correct initial value (e.g., 1 for root or passed from parent). The condition `if(p->h>1000)` is meaningless because p->h contains garbage.]

40.reason: [Incorrect height assignment: The height of the child is set after recursive insertion, but if the inserted node is deep in the tree, this overwrites heights incorrectly. Also, if p->h itself was never initialized (as in root), this propagates garbage. Height should be passed during insertion, not assigned afterward.]
